name: New York Common Pantry
hours: Check website before visiting, hours vary by program
eligibility: Open to all NYC residents. No ID required for emergency food.
notes: One of NYC's largest food pantries. Operates a client-choice model in which guests shop like in a grocery store, selecting foods that fit their family's needs and culture.

name: Coalition for the Homeless
hours: Grand Central Food Program: nightly 7:00 pm – 10:00 pm (year-round)
eligibility: No requirements, open to anyone who is hungry or unhoused.
notes: The Grand Central Food Program has served meals every night without exception for over 40 years. No registration or ID required. Meals are distributed near Grand Central Terminal.

name: City Harvest
hours: Mobile markets: check cityharvest.org/get-help/find-food for current locations and times
eligibility: No requirements at mobile markets. Just show up.
notes: City Harvest rescues millions of pounds of surplus food each year from restaurants, farms, and grocery stores, then distributes through a network of over 600 community food programs across NYC.

name: Lenox Hill Neighborhood House
hours: Check website before visiting
eligibility: Open to Upper East Side and East Harlem residents. Photo ID and proof of address are helpful but not required for first visit.
notes: A century-old settlement house offering integrated social services alongside food support. Known for its warmth and community-centered approach.

name: The Bowery Mission
hours: Meals served daily, check bowery.org for current service times
eligibility: No requirements for meals. Walk in anytime meals are being served. Shelter and recovery programs have an intake process.
notes: Serving New York's most vulnerable since 1879. Walk-in meals are available daily with no registration required. A place where everyone is welcomed with dignity.

name: Food Bank For New York City
hours: Check foodbanknyc.org/get-help/find-food for community partner hours near you
eligibility: No requirements at food distribution sites. SNAP assistance available for eligible households.
notes: The Food Bank operates NYC's largest hunger-relief network, supplying over 800 community food programs citywide. Their online Find Food tool helps you locate the nearest open pantry or kitchen.

name: West Side Campaign Against Hunger
hours: Check website before visiting, appointment system may be in use
eligibility: Open to anyone on Manhattan's west side. Some appointments required, call or check website.
notes: Uses a client-choice model: guests shop like in a grocery store, selecting items that fit their family's dietary needs and cultural preferences. A dignified, respectful approach to food assistance.

name: Holy Apostles Soup Kitchen
hours: Monday – Friday, lunch service, check website for current times
eligibility: No requirements. Open to all who are hungry.
notes: One of NYC's largest soup kitchens, serving up to 1,100 hot meals per day. Run almost entirely by volunteers inside an Episcopal church. A community gathering place as much as a meal program.

name: St. John's Bread & Life
hours: Check website before visiting
eligibility: Open to anyone in need. No documentation required for meals.
notes: Brooklyn's largest soup kitchen and food pantry, serving the Bushwick and Bed-Stuy communities. A stable, trusted presence in a neighborhood that needs it.

name: Part of the Solution (POTS)
hours: Check website before visiting
eligibility: Open to all Bronx residents. Pantry may have neighborhood preference.
notes: A beloved Bronx institution in the Fordham Heights community offering food alongside wraparound services, case management, legal help, and connections to housing and healthcare.

name: Xavier Mission
hours: Saturday and Sunday meals, check website for current times
eligibility: No requirements. Open to all.
notes: A welcoming community that has served weekend meals since 1993. Known for its warmth and dignity-centered approach, guests are neighbors, not numbers.

name: CHiPS Soup Kitchen & Women's Shelter
hours: Six days a week, check chipsonline.org for current meal hours
eligibility: Soup kitchen open to all. Shelter is for women experiencing homelessness.
notes: One of Brooklyn's longest-running soup kitchens and an emergency women's shelter, serving Park Slope and surrounding neighborhoods since 1971. Serves over 190,000 meals per year.

name: Rethink Food
hours: Check rethinkfood.org for current distribution sites and hours
eligibility: No requirements at distribution sites.
notes: Founded by a Michelin-starred chef, Rethink Food partners with restaurants and food businesses to rescue surplus food and distribute nutritious, restaurant-quality meals to communities across NYC.

name: God's Love We Deliver
address: 166 Avenue of the Americas, New York, NY 10013
hours: Deliveries on weekdays, call to apply for service
eligibility: For people living with serious illness (HIV/AIDS, cancer, heart disease, and others). Free of charge. Call to apply, a brief intake process confirms eligibility.
notes: Provides medically tailored meals, developed by registered dietitians, to people who are too ill to shop or cook. Serving New York City since 1985. Completely free, no income requirement.

name: Encore Community Services
hours: Congregate lunch Mon–Fri 11:30am–2:30pm; home-delivered meals on Manhattan's west side
eligibility: Primarily for adults 60+ in Manhattan. Call to learn about programs and eligibility.
notes: Dedicated to older New Yorkers since 1977, Encore provides hot meals in a warm community setting and delivers to homebound seniors on the West Side of Manhattan. Meals are accompanied by social connection, a crucial part of the program.

name: Met Council
address: 1 State Street Plaza, New York, NY 10004
hours: Call or visit metcouncil.org to find the nearest pantry location and hours
eligibility: Open to all regardless of religion or background. Call (212) 453-9539 or email food@metcouncil.org to connect with the nearest program.
notes: One of NYC's largest emergency food networks, serving over 150,000 people per month through nearly 200 partner pantries and direct programs citywide. Serves all backgrounds and faiths, with strong reach in Brooklyn, Queens, and Manhattan.

name: The Bowery Mission – Tribeca Campus
hours: Meals daily: breakfast 8–9am, lunch 1–2pm, dinner 5:30–6:30pm
eligibility: No requirements for meals. Shelter and recovery programs have an intake process.
notes: Formerly the NYC Rescue Mission, now The Bowery Mission's Tribeca Campus. Serves the Tribeca and Lower Manhattan community with three daily meals and emergency shelter. Operating at this location since 1872.

name: Citymeals on Wheels
hours: Deliveries on weekdays and weekends, call or apply online
eligibility: For NYC residents age 60+ who are homebound and cannot prepare their own meals. Free of charge.
notes: Founded in 1981, Citymeals delivers over 2 million meals a year to more than 18,000 frail and elderly New Yorkers who are unable to leave their homes. One of the city's most respected senior meal programs.

name: Masbia Soup Kitchen Network
hours: Check masbia.org for current hours at each location
eligibility: No requirements. Open to all, kosher meals served with full dignity.
notes: One of the largest kosher soup kitchen networks in the United States, with locations in Rego Park (Queens), Borough Park, and Flatbush (Brooklyn). Serves thousands of meals weekly with no questions asked.

name: BronxWorks
hours: Check bronxworks.org for food program hours and locations
eligibility: Open to Bronx residents. No documentation required for emergency food.
notes: One of the Bronx's most comprehensive social services organizations, BronxWorks runs food pantries and meal programs alongside housing, workforce, and senior services. A cornerstone institution of the borough.

name: Project Hospitality
hours: Check website before visiting
eligibility: No requirements for meals. Open to all Staten Island residents.
notes: Staten Island's leading hunger-relief and social services organization. Project Hospitality has served the borough's most vulnerable residents since 1982 through meals, shelter, and wraparound support.

name: The Campaign Against Hunger
hours: Check tcahnyc.org for current pantry hours at each location
eligibility: Open to Brooklyn residents. Photo ID and proof of Brooklyn address helpful.
notes: One of Brooklyn's most active food pantries, serving Bed-Stuy, East New York, and Ocean Hill with a supermarket-style pantry open five days a week. Known for its client-choice model, fresh produce, and on-site urban farm.

name: Queens Community House
hours: Food pantry Thursdays 8:30am–10:30am; call for other program schedules
eligibility: Open to Queens residents. Call or check website for specific program eligibility.
notes: A cornerstone of Queens for over 90 years, Queens Community House operates food pantries and senior meal programs alongside a full suite of community services across multiple Queens neighborhoods.

name: Neighbors Together
hours: Check neighborstogether.org for current pantry and meal service hours
eligibility: Open to all Brooklyn residents. Call ahead or check website for current requirements.
notes: A Brooklyn institution serving Bed-Stuy and East New York since 1982. Combines a food pantry and hot meal program with social services and grassroots advocacy for food justice.

name: Commonpoint Queens
hours: Mon–Fri 9am–6:30pm, Sun 10am–2pm (Forest Hills); check commonpoint.org for all locations
eligibility: Open to Queens residents. Register online or call to become a client. Free of charge.
notes: A major Queens community organization operating food pantries at multiple sites across Forest Hills, Queens Village, and beyond. Delivers over 130,000 meals to homebound seniors and uses a digital pantry system so clients can pre-order their food selections.
